questions.question:
Four persons P, Q, R and S are to be seated in a row, all facing the same direction, but not necessarily in the same order. P and R cannot sit adjacent to each other. S should be seated to the right of Q. The number of distinct seating arrangements possible is:

A. 4   B. 4   C. 6   D. 8
questions.to_find: Number of distinct seating arrangements of P, Q, R, S with P and R non-adjacent and S to the right of Q

understand.plain: We have 4 people P, Q, R, S to be arranged in a row of 4 seats. Two constraints apply simultaneously: (1) P and R must not sit next to each other, and (2) S must appear somewhere to the right of Q (not necessarily immediately right). We count total valid arrangements using complementary counting.

keywords.explain: Two people are adjacent when they occupy consecutive seats. If P and R are adjacent, their seat numbers differ by exactly 1.
keywords.example: In arrangement PRQS, P is in seat 1 and R is in seat 2, so they are adjacent — this violates the constraint.

keywords.explain: S must be to the right of Q means S's seat number is greater than Q's seat number. They need not be adjacent.
keywords.example: In QPRS, Q is at seat 1 and S is at seat 4 — S is to the right of Q, so this satisfies the constraint.

keywords.explain: Count the unrestricted set, then subtract invalid cases. First halve the total by symmetry to enforce S-right-of-Q, then subtract the P-R adjacent cases from that half.
keywords.example: Half of 4! = 12 arrangements have S after Q. Among these 12, subtract the ones where P and R are also adjacent.

understand.visual_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 460 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='10' y='20' width='90' height='50' rx='6' class='bg'/><text x='55' y='52' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Seat 1</text><rect x='120' y='20' width='90' height='50' rx='6' class='bg'/><text x='165' y='52' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Seat 2</text><rect x='230' y='20' width='90' height='50' rx='6' class='bg'/><text x='275' y='52' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Seat 3</text><rect x='340' y='20' width='90' height='50' rx='6' class='bg'/><text x='385' y='52' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Seat 4</text><text x='230' y='95' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>pos(S) > pos(Q), and P and R must not be in adjacent seats</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Four seats in a row numbered 1 to 4; S must occupy a higher-numbered seat than Q, and P and R must not be in consecutive seats.

given.aim: Count arrangements of P, Q, R, S in 4 seats where P and R are not adjacent and S is to the right of Q

terms.meaning: 4! = 24 ways to arrange 4 distinct people in 4 seats with no restrictions
terms.example: PQRS, PQSR, PRQS, ... are all 24 permutations before applying constraints
terms.connects: Starting point for both complementary-counting steps

terms.meaning: For every arrangement, exactly one of (Q left of S) or (S left of Q) holds, so exactly 24/2 = 12 arrangements have S to the right of Q
terms.example: PQSR has Q at 2, S at 3 (S after Q); swapping gives PSQR where Q at 3, S at 2 (Q after S)
terms.connects: Reduces the working set from 24 to 12

terms.meaning: Treat {P, R} as a single block (with 2 internal orders: PR or RP), giving 3 units. Among the 12 valid arrangements, those where P and R are adjacent must be removed.
terms.example: Block {PR} + Q + S: 3 units with 2 internal block orders; those with S after Q = 3!/2 * 2 = 6
terms.connects: These 6 forbidden cases are subtracted from 12

given.plan: Step 1: Valid arrangements with S after Q = 4!/2 = 12. Step 2: Among these, count arrangements where P and R are also adjacent. Treat {PR} as a block (2 internal orders): 3 units, half have S after Q giving 3!/2 = 3 per internal order, 3*2 = 6 total. Step 3: Answer = 12 - 6 = 6.

steps.title: Count arrangements with S to the right of Q
steps.apply: Total arrangements = 4! = 24. By symmetry between Q and S, exactly half have S after Q: 24 / 2 = 12.
steps.note: For any arrangement, exactly one of the two relative orderings of Q and S holds.

steps.title: Count forbidden arrangements (S after Q AND P adjacent to R)
steps.formula_raw: Treat adjacent pair as one block, then apply symmetry
steps.apply: Treat {P, R} as one block (2 internal orders: PR or RP). We now have 3 units: {block}, Q, S. Total arrangements of 3 units = 3! = 6. With S after Q (by symmetry): 6/2 = 3 per internal order. With 2 internal orders: 3 * 2 = 6 forbidden arrangements.
steps.note: Each internal block order (PR or RP) contributes 3 arrangements where S is after Q.

steps.title: Subtract forbidden cases
steps.formula_raw: |valid| = |S after Q| - |S after Q AND P adj R|
steps.note: The answer is 6, matching option C.

questions.formula_note: Symmetry halves the total to enforce S-after-Q; complementary counting with a block argument removes the P-adjacent-to-R cases.

questions.question:
Four persons P, Q, R and S are to be seated in a row. R should not be seated at the second position from the left end of the row. The number of distinct seating arrangements possible is:

A. 6   B. 9   C. 18   D. 24
questions.to_find: Number of distinct seating arrangements of P, Q, R, S where R does not occupy seat 2

understand.plain: Four distinct people P, Q, R, S sit in a row of 4 seats numbered 1 to 4 from left to right. The only constraint is that R must not sit at seat 2 (second from the left). We use complementary counting: start from all 4! = 24 arrangements and subtract those where R is placed at seat 2.

keywords.explain: One specific seat (seat 2) is forbidden for one specific person (R). All other 3 seats are available to R, and all seats are open to the other three people.
keywords.example: R can sit at seat 1, 3, or 4 — just not seat 2.

keywords.explain: Instead of counting valid arrangements directly, count invalid ones (R at seat 2) and subtract from total.
When R is fixed at seat 2, the remaining 3 people (P, Q, S) fill the remaining 3 seats freely in 3! ways.
keywords.example: P, Q, S can arrange themselves in seats 1, 3, 4 in 3! = 6 ways when R is locked at seat 2.

understand.visual_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 460 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='10' y='20' width='90' height='55' rx='6' class='bg'/><text x='55' y='54' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Seat 1</text><rect x='120' y='20' width='90' height='55' rx='6' style='fill:var(--no,#D85A30);stroke:var(--no,#D85A30);stroke-width:3;'/><text x='165' y='43' text-anchor='middle' class='tag' style='fill:#fff;'>Seat 2</text><text x='165' y='66' text-anchor='middle' class='sub' style='fill:#fff;'>R not here</text><rect x='230' y='20' width='90' height='55' rx='6' class='bg'/><text x='275' y='54' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Seat 3</text><rect x='340' y='20' width='90' height='55' rx='6' class='bg'/><text x='385' y='54' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Seat 4</text><text x='230' y='98' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>R can sit anywhere except seat 2</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Four seats in a row; seat 2 is highlighted red indicating R is forbidden from that position.

given.aim: Count all row arrangements of P, Q, R, S where R does not sit at seat 2

terms.meaning: 4! = 24; all ways to assign 4 distinct people to 4 seats without any restriction
terms.example: PQRS, PQSR, RPQS, etc. — all 24 orderings are counted before filtering
terms.connects: The base count from which the forbidden cases are subtracted

terms.meaning: Fix R at seat 2; the other 3 people (P, Q, S) freely fill the 3 remaining seats in 3! = 6 ways
terms.example: PRQS, QRPS, SRQP, PRQS, QRSP, SRPS — exactly 6 invalid arrangements with R at seat 2
terms.connects: This count is subtracted to enforce the constraint

terms.meaning: Total minus forbidden = 24 - 6 = 18 arrangements where R is at seat 1, 3, or 4
terms.example: RPQS (R at seat 1), PQRS (R at seat 4), PRSQ (R at seat 3) are all valid

given.plan: Valid = total - forbidden. Total = 4! = 24. Forbidden (R at seat 2) = 3! = 6. Answer = 24 - 6 = 18.

steps.title: Count arrangements where R is at seat 2 (forbidden cases)
steps.formula_raw: (n-1)! arrangements when one person is fixed
steps.apply: Fix R at seat 2. Remaining 3 people P, Q, S fill seats 1, 3, 4 freely: 3! = 6 ways.
steps.note: All 6 of these are invalid — they must be removed.

questions.formula_note: One forbidden seat for one person: subtract the (n-1)! fixed-position arrangements from the n! total.

questions.question:
Five persons P, Q, R, S and T are sitting in a row, not necessarily in the same order. Q and R are separated by one person, and S should not be seated adjacent to Q. The number of distinct seating arrangements possible is:

A. 8   B. 8   C. 10   D. 16
questions.to_find: Number of distinct seating arrangements of P, Q, R, S, T where Q and R have exactly one person between them and S is not adjacent to Q

understand.plain: Five people P, Q, R, S, T sit in a row of 5 seats. Constraint 1: Q and R are separated by exactly one seat (one person sits between them), meaning |pos(Q) - pos(R)| = 2. Constraint 2: S must not sit immediately next to Q. We enumerate all valid (Q, R) placements and count valid completions with the S restriction.

keywords.explain: Q and R have exactly one seat between them, so their position numbers differ by 2. The person in the middle seat is the separator.
keywords.example: Q at seat 1 and R at seat 3: seat 2 holds the separating person. This satisfies |pos(Q) - pos(R)| = 2.

keywords.explain: S is not adjacent to Q when |pos(S) - pos(Q)| != 1, i.e., S does not sit in the seat immediately before or after Q.
keywords.example: If Q is at seat 3, S cannot be at seat 2 or seat 4.

keywords.explain: List all 6 ordered (Q, R) position pairs with |pos(Q) - pos(R)| = 2, then for each case count how many arrangements of the remaining 3 people satisfy the S-not-adjacent-to-Q rule.

understand.visual_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 580 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='10' y='20' width='100' height='55' rx='6' class='hi'/><text x='60' y='44' text-anchor='middle' class='tag ink'>Seat 1</text><text x='60' y='65' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Q</text><rect x='120' y='20' width='100' height='55' rx='6' class='bg'/><text x='170' y='44' text-anchor='middle' class='tag ink'>Seat 2</text><text x='170' y='65' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>gap</text><rect x='230' y='20' width='100' height='55' rx='6' class='hi'/><text x='280' y='44' text-anchor='middle' class='tag ink'>Seat 3</text><text x='280' y='65' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>R</text><rect x='340' y='20' width='100' height='55' rx='6' class='bg'/><text x='390' y='44' text-anchor='middle' class='tag ink'>Seat 4</text><rect x='450' y='20' width='100' height='55' rx='6' class='bg'/><text x='500' y='44' text-anchor='middle' class='tag ink'>Seat 5</text><text x='290' y='108' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>Example: Q at 1, one person at 2 (gap), R at 3; S cannot be at seat 2</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Five seats with Q at seat 1 and R at seat 3 (both purple) showing one gap seat between them; remaining seats hold P, S, T with S forbidden from being next to Q.

given.aim: Count arrangements where Q and R have exactly one person between them, and S is not adjacent to Q

terms.term: Valid (Q, R) ordered pairs with gap = 1
terms.meaning: All ordered position pairs where |pos(Q) - pos(R)| = 2 in a 5-seat row: (1,3),(2,4),(3,5),(3,1),(4,2),(5,3) — exactly 6 pairs
terms.example: (Q=1, R=3) means Q at seat 1 and R at seat 3 with the gap at seat 2
terms.connects: Six cases to evaluate; Q and R are distinct people so order matters

terms.term: Remaining 3 people {P, S, T} in 3 seats
terms.meaning: After fixing Q and R, the 3 remaining seats are filled by P, S, T. For each (Q, R) placement, identify which seats are adjacent to Q so S can be excluded from them.
terms.example: If Q=2, adjacent seats are 1 and 3. S cannot be placed at 1 or 3.
terms.connects: Drives the per-case count of valid completions

terms.meaning: S cannot be at any seat with position number exactly 1 away from Q's position. The count of forbidden seats for S varies by case (Q at an end has 1 adjacent; Q in the middle has 2).
terms.example: Q at seat 1 has only seat 2 adjacent; Q at seat 3 has seats 2 and 4 adjacent.
terms.connects: Used to compute valid completions: 3! minus arrangements with S in a Q-adjacent seat

given.plan: Enumerate all 6 ordered (Q, R) pairs. For each: identify Q's adjacent seats, count arrangements of {P, S, T} in the 3 remaining seats where S avoids Q's adjacent seats. Sum all valid counts.

steps.title: List all (Q, R) placements separated by exactly one person
steps.apply: Valid ordered pairs (Q position, R position): (1,3), (2,4), (3,5), (3,1), (4,2), (5,3). That is 6 cases. For each, the gap seat is the middle of Q and R.
steps.note: Q and R are distinct so (Q=1,R=3) and (Q=3,R=1) are different arrangements.

steps.title: Count valid completions for each of the 6 cases
steps.formula_raw: For each case: valid = 3! - (arrangements with S at Q-adjacent seat)
steps.apply: Case (Q=1,R=3): gap at 2; remaining seats {2,4,5} for {P,S,T}; Q adjacent to seat 2 only (seat 0 doesn't exist). S cannot be at seat 2: 3! - 2! = 6 - 2 = 4. Case (Q=2,R=4): gap at 3; remaining seats {1,3,5} for {P,S,T}; Q adjacent to seats 1 and 3. S can only go to seat 5; P,T fill {1,3} in 2! = 2 ways. Valid = 2. Case (Q=3,R=5): gap at 4; remaining seats {1,2,4} for {P,S,T}; Q adjacent to seats 2 and 4. S can only go to seat 1; P,T fill {2,4} in 2 ways. Valid = 2. Case (Q=3,R=1): gap at 2; remaining seats {2,4,5} for {P,S,T}; Q adjacent to seats 2 and 4. S cannot be at 2 or 4, so S must be at 5; P,T fill {2,4} in 2 ways. Valid = 2. Case (Q=4,R=2): gap at 3; remaining seats {1,3,5} for {P,S,T}; Q adjacent to seats 3 and 5. S cannot be at 3 or 5, so S must be at 1; P,T fill {3,5} in 2 ways. Valid = 2. Case (Q=5,R=3): gap at 4; remaining seats {1,2,4} for {P,S,T}; Q adjacent to seat 4 only (seat 6 doesn't exist). S cannot be at 4; S can be at 1 or 2 (2 choices); for each, P and T fill the remaining 2 seats in 2! = 2 ways. Valid = 2 * 2 = 4.
steps.note: Cases with Q at an end seat (seats 1 or 5) have only one adjacent seat, yielding 4 valid completions each. Cases with Q in the interior have two adjacent seats, yielding only 2 valid completions each.

steps.title: Sum all valid counts
steps.formula_raw: Total valid = sum across all cases

questions.formula_note: Case-by-case enumeration over the 6 ordered (Q,R) gap-placements; for each, complement counting removes S-adjacent-to-Q configurations.

questions.question:
Five persons P, Q, R, S and T are to be seated in a row, all facing the same direction, but not necessarily in the same order. P and T cannot be seated at either end of the row. S should not be seated adjacent to P. R is to be seated at the second position from the left end of the row. The number of distinct seating arrangements possible is:

A. 2   B. 3   C. 4   D. 5
questions.to_find: Number of distinct seating arrangements of P, Q, R, S, T with R fixed at seat 2, P and T not at ends, and S not adjacent to P

understand.plain: Five people P, Q, R, S, T sit in seats 1 through 5. R is fixed at seat 2. P and T cannot be at the ends (seats 1 or 5). S must not sit immediately next to P. With R locked, we arrange {P, Q, S, T} in seats {1, 3, 4, 5} under these constraints.

keywords.explain: R is placed at seat 2 as given. This removes seat 2 from the arrangement pool and reduces the problem to placing {P, Q, S, T} in seats {1, 3, 4, 5}.
keywords.example: The row looks like [?][R][?][?][?] — R is locked, 4 seats remain for 4 people.

keywords.term: End-seat restriction for P and T
keywords.explain: Seats 1 and 5 are the ends of the row. Neither P nor T can occupy these seats.
keywords.example: P and T must sit in seats 3 or 4. They cannot sit at seat 1 or seat 5.

keywords.explain: S cannot occupy the seat immediately to the left or right of P. Once P's seat is chosen, S must avoid the neighboring seats.
keywords.example: If P is at seat 3, S cannot be at seat 4 (seat 2 is taken by R, so no left-neighbor issue from seat 3).

understand.visual_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 580 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='10' y='20' width='100' height='60' rx='6' style='fill:var(--no,#D85A30);stroke:var(--no,#D85A30);stroke-width:2;'/><text x='60' y='44' text-anchor='middle' class='tag' style='fill:#fff;'>Seat 1</text><text x='60' y='68' text-anchor='middle' class='sub' style='fill:#fff;'>No P or T</text><rect x='120' y='20' width='100' height='60' rx='6' class='hi'/><text x='170' y='44' text-anchor='middle' class='tag ink'>Seat 2</text><text x='170' y='68' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>R fixed</text><rect x='230' y='20' width='100' height='60' rx='6' class='ok'/><text x='280' y='44' text-anchor='middle' class='tag' style='fill:#fff;'>Seat 3</text><text x='280' y='68' text-anchor='middle' class='sub' style='fill:#fff;'>P or T ok</text><rect x='340' y='20' width='100' height='60' rx='6' class='ok'/><text x='390' y='44' text-anchor='middle' class='tag' style='fill:#fff;'>Seat 4</text><text x='390' y='68' text-anchor='middle' class='sub' style='fill:#fff;'>P or T ok</text><rect x='450' y='20' width='100' height='60' rx='6' style='fill:var(--no,#D85A30);stroke:var(--no,#D85A30);stroke-width:2;'/><text x='500' y='44' text-anchor='middle' class='tag' style='fill:#fff;'>Seat 5</text><text x='500' y='68' text-anchor='middle' class='sub' style='fill:#fff;'>No P or T</text><text x='290' y='120' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>R fixed at 2; P and T restricted to seats 3,4; S not adjacent to P</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Five seats with R fixed at seat 2 (purple); seats 1 and 5 marked red (no P or T); seats 3 and 4 marked green (available for P and T).

given.aim: Count valid arrangements with R at seat 2, P and T not at seats 1 or 5, and S not adjacent to P

terms.term: R fixed at seat 2; remaining people and seats
terms.meaning: R occupies seat 2. We arrange {P, Q, S, T} in seats {1, 3, 4, 5}.
terms.example: Seats 1, 3, 4, 5 are available; P and T cannot use seats 1 and 5.
terms.connects: Reduces the 5! problem to a 4-seat constrained assignment

terms.term: P and T forced to seats {3, 4}
terms.meaning: Since P and T cannot be at seat 1 or seat 5, and seats {1,3,4,5} are available, P and T must occupy seats 3 and 4 (the only non-end seats available).
terms.example: P at seat 3 and T at seat 4, or T at seat 3 and P at seat 4 — 2 ways to assign P and T.
terms.connects: After placing P and T, seats 1 and 5 go to Q and S

terms.term: S not adjacent to P — filtering the 2 (P,T) placements
terms.meaning: Depending on which seat P occupies, S may or may not be allowed next to P. Check each of the 2 P-T assignments combined with each of the 2 Q-S assignments for validity.
terms.example: If P=3: S cannot be at seat 4 (|4-3|=1). So S must be at seat 1 or seat 5 — but those are only available if Q takes the other end seat. Check each sub-case.
terms.connects: Eliminates invalid configurations from the 2*2 = 4 candidate arrangements

given.plan: Fix R at seat 2. P and T must go to seats {3,4}: 2 arrangements (P-T or T-P). Q and S fill seats {1,5}: 2 arrangements (Q at 1 and S at 5, or S at 1 and Q at 5). For each of the 4 combinations, check if S is adjacent to P and discard if so. Count remaining valid combinations.

steps.title: Fix R at seat 2; determine forced placements
steps.apply: R is at seat 2. Remaining: {P, Q, S, T} in seats {1, 3, 4, 5}. P and T cannot be at ends (seats 1 or 5). The only non-end available seats are 3 and 4. So P and T must occupy seats 3 and 4 in some order: (P=3,T=4) or (P=4,T=3). Q and S then fill seats 1 and 5.
steps.note: This gives 2 * 2 = 4 candidate arrangements before applying the S-not-adjacent-to-P rule.

steps.title: Check each candidate against S-not-adjacent-to-P
steps.apply: Candidate 1: P=3, T=4, Q=1, S=5. |pos(S)-pos(P)| = |5-3| = 2. S not adjacent to P. Valid. Candidate 2: P=3, T=4, Q=5, S=1. |pos(S)-pos(P)| = |1-3| = 2. S not adjacent to P. Valid. Candidate 3: P=4, T=3, Q=1, S=5. |pos(S)-pos(P)| = |5-4| = 1. S IS adjacent to P. Invalid. Candidate 4: P=4, T=3, Q=5, S=1. |pos(S)-pos(P)| = |1-4| = 3. S not adjacent to P. Valid.
steps.note: Candidate 3 is rejected because S at seat 5 and P at seat 4 are adjacent.

steps.title: Count valid arrangements
steps.apply: Valid arrangements: Candidate 1 (Q-R-P-T-S), Candidate 2 (S-R-P-T-Q), Candidate 4 (S-R-T-P-Q). Total = 3.

solution.result: 3 distinct seating arrangements (Option B): Q-R-P-T-S, S-R-P-T-Q, S-R-T-P-Q

questions.formula_note: Fixed R and end restrictions force P, T to seats 3 and 4; the S-not-adjacent-to-P check eliminates exactly one of the four candidate arrangements, leaving 3.
